title: Poached Butter Brazilian Stir-fry
description: A creamy and indulgent Brazilian dish featuring tabasco sauce for a spicy kick, served as a light lunch or dinner option.
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine olive oil with the onions and garlic. Sauté until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add bell peppers and cubed chicken or tofu; stir for another 3-4 minutes. Season lightly with salt.
Pour in low-sodium broth and tabasco sauce. Mix well to combine. Bring to a boil over medium heat.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until liquid is reduced by half (about 10-15 minutes). Stir occasionally.
In another pan, melt butter with flour for about 30 seconds, stirring continuously. Slowly pour in the poaching mixture while whisking vigorously to emulsify.
Return sauce to low heat and simmer until thickened slightly (approx. 2-3 more minutes).
Strain through a fine mesh sieve into serving dishes or bowls. Garnish with fresh parsley before serving.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course, optionally with crusty bread for dipping.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
